title: [FIRRTL] `automatic logic` in Verilog2001 output
body:
FIRTOOL produces `automatic logic` statements inside Verilog2001 output.
We need a way to produce Verilog2001 compatible code without SystemVerilog constructs in it.

The following FIRRTL program

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    input clock : Clock
    input foo : UInt<16>
    output baz0 : UInt<16>
    output baz1 : UInt<16>
    reg bar : UInt<16>, clock
    bar <= foo
    baz0 <= bar
    baz1 <= bar
```

Compiled with `firtool` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
...
module top_mod(
  input         clock,
  input  [15:0] foo,
  output [15:0] baz0, baz1);

  reg [15:0] bar;	// examples/reassign.fir:7:5

  `ifndef SYNTHESIS	// examples/reassign.fir:7:5
    initial begin	// examples/reassign.fir:7:5
      automatic logic [31:0] _T_0;	// examples/reassign.fir:7:5

      `INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_	// examples/reassign.fir:7:5
      `ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT	// examples/reassign.fir:7:5
        _T_0 = `RANDOM;	// examples/reassign.fir:7:5
        bar = _T_0[15:0];	// examples/reassign.fir:7:5
      `endif
    end // initial
  `endif
  always @(posedge clock)	// examples/reassign.fir:8:9
    bar <= foo;	// examples/reassign.fir:8:9
  wire [15:0] _T = bar;	// examples/reassign.fir:2:10
  assign baz0 = _T;	// examples/reassign.fir:2:10
  assign baz1 = _T;	// examples/reassign.fir:2:10
endmodule
```
